nodes.name: testFile
nodes.description: Path to the init command file used as the extraction test target.

nodes.name: result
nodes.description: Extraction result containing all symbols found in the test file.

nodes.name: initCommand
nodes.description: Single-symbol extraction test targeting `registerInitCommand`.

nodes.name: StaleChecker
nodes.description: Detects when graph.json is out of sync with source code by comparing content hashes.

nodes.name: registerCheckCommand
nodes.description:
Register the `treck check` CLI command.

Checks graph.json freshness by comparing stored hashes against current
source code. Exits with code 1 if any stale nodes are found (useful for CI).

nodes.name: formatStaleReason
nodes.description: Map a staleness reason code to a human-readable label.

nodes.name: registerInitCommand
nodes.description:
Register the `treck init` CLI command.

Runs an interactive setup wizard that prompts for output directory,
include/exclude patterns, and writes a `config.yaml` file.

nodes.name: splitGlobPatterns
nodes.description:
Split a comma-separated glob list while preserving commas inside brace sets.
Example: `<pattern-1>,<pattern-2>` => two patterns.

nodes.name: generateConfigYAML
nodes.description: Serialize an init config object to a YAML config file string.

nodes.name: registerJsDocCommand
nodes.description:
Register the `treck jsdoc` CLI command.

Without flags: scans the project and reports JSDoc coverage with a list
of symbols missing comments.

With `--prompt`: prints a ready-to-use agent prompt to stdout for piping
into coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, or Cursor.

With `--run <agent>`: spawns the named agent CLI with the prompt directly.
Supported agents: claude, codex.

nodes.name: registerServeCommand
nodes.description:
Register the `treck serve` CLI command.

Starts the documentation viewer HTTP server and optionally opens it
in the default browser. Supports `--focus` to open with specific
symbols or files pre-selected and focused.

nodes.name: buildMetadataLine
nodes.description:
Build a metadata header line for a node in --docs output.

Shows file path, kind, line range, and entry type info on a single line.

structuredParams.name: node

nodes.name: registerShowCommand
nodes.description:
Register the `treck show` CLI command.

Outputs graph data to stdout in mermaid (default) or markdown format.
Targets are required positional args specifying which symbols to show.

nodes.name: beautifyMermaid
nodes.description:
Render a mermaid diagram as Unicode box-drawing art for terminal display.

Uses `beautiful-mermaid`'s ASCII renderer under the hood.

nodes.name: formatMermaidOutput
nodes.description:
Format mermaid graph output for the given node IDs.

Single target produces a per-node diagram. Multiple targets produce
a combined diagram with all targets highlighted.

nodes.name: formatDocsOutput
nodes.description:
Format markdown documentation output for the given node IDs.

Each symbol gets a metadata header, rendered markdown body, and
an embedded mermaid diagram. Multiple symbols are separated by `---`.

nodes.name: registerSyncCommand
nodes.description:
Register the `treck sync` CLI command.

Finds source files, builds the dependency graph, and writes graph.json.
Optionally filters to a target path.

nodes.name: cli
nodes.description: The treck CLI instance, powered by cac.

nodes.name: parsed
nodes.description: Parsed CLI arguments and options.

nodes.name: loadConfig
nodes.description:
Load the treck configuration from `_treck/config.yaml`.

Parses the YAML config file to extract the output directory and
include/exclude scope patterns. Returns null if no config file exists.

structuredParams.name: cwd
structuredParams.description: Working directory to resolve the config path from

nodes.name: parseYamlList
nodes.description:
Parse a YAML list under a given key.
Finds `key:` on its own line, then collects subsequent `- value` lines.
Skips blank lines and comment lines within the list.

nodes.name: stripQuotes
nodes.description: Remove surrounding single or double quotes from a string value.

nodes.name: detectIncludePatterns
nodes.description:
Detect likely source-code include patterns from common project layouts.

Checks for workspace configs (pnpm-workspace.yaml, package.json workspaces)
first to handle monorepos, then falls back to scanning hardcoded candidate
directories. Returns a fallback placeholder when nothing is found.

nodes.name: detectWorkspacePatterns
nodes.description:
Read workspace package directories from pnpm-workspace.yaml or package.json
workspaces, resolve globs, and return include patterns for directories that
contain source files. Returns `null` when no workspace config is found.

nodes.name: readWorkspacePackages
nodes.description:
Read the raw workspace package entries from pnpm-workspace.yaml or
package.json. Returns `null` if neither config exists.

nodes.name: parsePnpmWorkspaceYaml
nodes.description:
Parse the `packages` list from a pnpm-workspace.yaml string.

Handles the simple YAML subset used by pnpm workspace configs — a
`packages:` key followed by `- entry` list items.

nodes.name: resolveWorkspaceDirs
nodes.description:
Resolve workspace entries to concrete directory names.

Plain entries (e.g. `backend`) are returned as-is if the directory exists.
Glob entries ending with `/*` or `/**` are expanded by listing subdirectories
of the parent path. Negated entries (e.g. `!packages/internal`) are excluded
from the final result.

nodes.name: TreckError
nodes.description: Base error class for all treck errors.

nodes.name: ConfigError
nodes.description: Thrown when the treck configuration file is invalid or missing required fields.

nodes.name: ExtractionError
nodes.description: Thrown when symbol extraction from a source file fails.

nodes.name: extractFileSymbols
nodes.description:
Extract symbol metadata from a single file.

Returns an empty array if the file cannot be parsed.

nodes.name: buildScanResult
nodes.description: Compute coverage statistics from extracted symbols.

nodes.name: scanProject
nodes.description: Scan the project synchronously and return JSDoc coverage data.

nodes.name: tick
nodes.description: Yield to the event loop so spinner animations stay smooth during CPU-bound work.

nodes.name: scanProjectAsync
nodes.description: Scan the project asynchronously, yielding periodically so spinner animations stay smooth.

structuredParams.name: scope
structuredParams.description: Include/exclude patterns for source file discovery

structuredParams.name: onProgress
structuredParams.description: Optional callback invoked with progress messages

nodes.name: renderJsDocCoverageStats
nodes.description: Render JSDoc coverage stats (progress bar + percentage).

nodes.name: renderMissingJsDocList
nodes.description:
Render the list of symbols missing JSDoc comments, grouped by file.

Shows all symbols when `verbose` is true or when the total count is 20 or fewer.
Otherwise, prints a hint to use --verbose.

structuredParams.name: scan
structuredParams.description: Project scan result containing symbol data

structuredParams.name: verbose
structuredParams.description: Whether to force-show all symbols regardless of count

nodes.name: resolveSourcePath
nodes.description:
Resolve a source file path from doc frontmatter to an absolute path
in the current working directory. Used when reading source files.

nodes.name: resolveFocusTargets
nodes.description:
Resolve focus targets to graph node IDs.

Matches each target as an exact node ID first, then as a file path
(all symbols in that file). Unresolved targets are returned separately.

nodes.name: explainUnresolved
nodes.description:
Explain why a file path couldn't be resolved against the graph.

Checks whether the file is outside the configured scope (not matched
by include patterns, or matched by exclude patterns).

structuredParams.name: filePath
structuredParams.description: The file path portion of the unresolved target

structuredParams.name: config
structuredParams.description: The loaded treck config with scope patterns

nodes.name: findSourceFiles
nodes.description:
Find all source files matching the scope's include/exclude patterns.

Prefers `git ls-files` so gitignored files (e.g. generated code) are
automatically excluded. Falls back to a manual directory walk when not
inside a git repository.

nodes.name: findSourceFilesAsync
nodes.description:
/**
 * Async version of {@link findSourceFiles}that yields to the event loop
 * between I/O operations so spinner animations stay smooth.
 *
 * Prefers `git ls-files` so gitignored files are automatically excluded.
 * Falls back to a manual directory walk when not inside a git repository.

nodes.name: getRelativePath
nodes.description: Convert an absolute path to a path relative to the current working directory.

nodes.name: countImports
nodes.description:
Count how many times each source file is imported by other files.

Parses import/export statements from all source files, resolves relative
specifiers, and tallies import counts per file. Used to rank documentation
priority (most-imported files first).

structuredParams.name: sourceFiles
structuredParams.description: List of absolute source file paths

nodes.name: gitTrackedFilesSync
nodes.description:
List files known to git (tracked + untracked-but-not-ignored).

Returns relative paths. Returns `null` if not inside a git repository.

nodes.name: gitTrackedFilesAsync
nodes.description:
/**
 * Async version of {@link gitTrackedFilesSync}.

nodes.name: resolveImport
nodes.description:
Resolve a relative import specifier to an absolute file path.

Tries the exact path, then common extensions (`.ts`, `.tsx`, `.js`, `.jsx`),
then `index.*` variants. Only resolves to files in the `sourceFiles` set.

nodes.name: commentToString
nodes.description:
/**
 * Normalize a JSDoc comment value to a plain string.
 * The TS API returns either a string or a NodeArray<JSDocComment> depending on
 * whether the comment contains inline tags like {@link ...}.

nodes.name: extractJsDoc
nodes.description:
Extract JSDoc information from a TypeScript AST node.
Returns undefined if no JSDoc comment is found.

For VariableDeclarations (arrow functions, const assignments), the JSDoc is
attached to the parent VariableStatement, so we walk up the tree.

nodes.name: extractParamDescriptions
nodes.description: Extract

nodes.name: extractReturns
nodes.description: Extract

nodes.name: extractExamples
nodes.description: Extract all

nodes.name: extractDeprecated
nodes.description: Extract
nodes.deprecated:
tag. Returns true if present with no reason,
or the reason string if provided.

nodes.name: extractSeeTags
nodes.description: Extract
see:
tags. TheTS compiler parses these as JSDocSeeTag with a
special `name` property containing the reference, so we need dedicated handling.

nodes.name: extractAllTagTexts
nodes.description: Extract all occurrences of a given tag name (e.g. 'throws').

nodes.name: isTrivialBody
nodes.description:
Check whether a function body is trivial (just a return, no logic).

A trivial body contains only a single return statement with no preceding
declarations, hooks, control flow, or side effects. Examples: icon components
that return JSX, or pass-through wrappers that forward props.

structuredParams.name: body
structuredParams.description: The function body text as produced by the TypeScript extractor

nodes.name: TypeScriptExtractor
nodes.description: Extracts symbols (functions, classes, arrow functions, call expressions) from TypeScript/JavaScript files using the TS Compiler API.

nodes.name: pickExampleNodes
nodes.description:
Pick example nodes for the system prompt.

Selects entry points and the most-connected nodes to teach the AI the
ID format and give it a sense of the codebase structure.

nodes.name: buildSystemPrompt
nodes.description: Build the system prompt with example nodes and instructions.

nodes.name: executeSearchNodes
nodes.description: Execute a search_nodes tool call against the graph.

nodes.name: executeSelectNodes
nodes.description: Validate node IDs for a select_nodes tool call.

structuredParams.name: nodeIds
structuredParams.description: Array of node IDs to validate

nodes.name: connectionTypeToEdgeType
nodes.description:
Map a RuntimeConnection type to a GraphEdge EdgeType.
Falls back to 'async-dispatch' for unknown connection types.

nodes.name: isAsyncSymbol
nodes.description:
Check whether a symbol's fullText indicates it is async.
Matches `async function`, `async (`, `async =>`  etc.

nodes.name: GraphBuilder
nodes.description: /** Builds a {@link FlowGraph}from TypeScript source files by extracting symbols, detecting entry points, and resolving call edges.

nodes.name: entryPoints
nodes.description: Find all entry point nodes in the graph

nodes.name: reachableFrom
nodes.description:
Find all nodes reachable from a starting node via BFS.
Returns the subgraph (nodes + edges) reachable from the start.

nodes.name: pathsBetween
nodes.description:
Find all paths between two nodes using DFS.
Returns an array of paths, where each path is an array of node IDs.
Limits to maxPaths to avoid combinatorial explosion.

nodes.name: connectedSubgraph
nodes.description:
Find all nodes connected to the start nodes via bidirectional BFS.

Traverses both callers (incoming edges) and callees (outgoing edges)
up to `depth` hops from any start node. Returns the subgraph of all
reachable nodes and their connecting edges.

structuredParams.name: startNodeIds
structuredParams.description: One or more node IDs to start traversal from

structuredParams.name: depth
structuredParams.description: Maximum traversal depth (default: Infinity for full connected flow)

nodes.name: buildAdjacencyList
nodes.description: Build a forward adjacency list from graph edges (source → [targets]).

nodes.name: buildReverseAdjacencyList
nodes.description: Build a reverse adjacency list from graph edges (target → [sources]).

nodes.name: renderNodeMarkdown
nodes.description:
Render the markdown body for a graph node (no frontmatter).

Produces: title, description, parameters table, return type,
calls/called-by tables, examples, throws, and see-also sections.

structuredParams.name: graph
structuredParams.description: The full flow graph (needed for edge lookups)

nodes.name: renderEdgeTable
nodes.description: Render a table of edges showing connected nodes.

structuredParams.name: graph
structuredParams.description: The full graph (for node name lookups)

structuredParams.name: direction
structuredParams.description: Which end of the edge to show ('source' or 'target')

nodes.name: getDocPath
nodes.description:
Compute the virtual doc path for a graph node.

Mirrors the directory structure of the source file under the output dir.
Example: node with filePath "src/checker/index.ts" and name "StaleChecker"
produces "src/checker/index/StaleChecker.md".

nodes.name: nodeToMermaid
nodes.description:
Generate a mermaid flowchart for a specific node and its connections.

Traverses callers and callees up to `depth` hops from the target node
and renders them as a mermaid flowchart with the target highlighted.

structuredParams.name: nodeId
structuredParams.description: The node to center the diagram on

structuredParams.name: depth
structuredParams.description: Traversal depth (default: Infinity for full connected flow)

nodes.name: flowToMermaid
nodes.description:
Generate a mermaid flowchart for an entire flow.

Renders all provided nodes and edges as a mermaid flowchart.
Optionally highlights specific nodes (e.g. entry points or targets).

structuredParams.name: nodes
structuredParams.description: The nodes to include in the diagram

structuredParams.name: highlightIds
structuredParams.description: Set of node IDs to highlight (blue styling)

nodes.name: buildMermaid
nodes.description:
Build a mermaid flowchart string from a set of nodes and edges.

Groups nodes into subgraphs by file path, applies styling to
highlighted nodes and entry points, and renders edge arrows based on type.

nodes.name: formatNodeLabel
nodes.description: Build a display label for a graph node, including async prefix and metadata.

nodes.name: edgeArrow
nodes.description: Return the mermaid arrow syntax for a given edge type.

nodes.name: edgeTypeLabel
nodes.description: Return a label derived from the edge type, if applicable.

nodes.name: sanitizeLabel
nodes.description: Sanitize a label for mermaid (escape characters that break parsing)

nodes.name: sanitizeId
nodes.description: Sanitize a node ID for mermaid (replace special characters)

nodes.name: stripJsonComments
nodes.description:
Strip // and /* comments from JSON text without corrupting string literals.
Walks character-by-character, skipping over quoted strings so that
sequences like "@/*" inside paths are preserved.

nodes.name: loadTsconfigPaths
nodes.description:
Find and parse tsconfig.json paths from a source file's directory.
Walks up the directory tree until it finds a tsconfig.json.
Results are cached per tsconfig.json location.

nodes.name: tryResolveFile
nodes.description:
Try to resolve a file path by testing common extensions.
Handles .js → .ts extension swapping for TypeScript ESM projects.

nodes.name: resolveImportPath
nodes.description:
Resolve an import specifier to an absolute file path.

Handles:
- Relative imports (./foo, ../bar)
- tsconfig path aliases (@/lib/foo,

nodes.name: clearTsconfigCache
nodes.description: Clear the tsconfig cache (useful for testing)

nodes.name: dirnameBrowser
nodes.description:
Return the directory portion of a file path.

Browser-safe alternative to `path.dirname` from `node:path`.

nodes.name: buildSymbolIndexFromGraph
nodes.description:
Build a lookup index of all symbols from graph data.

Iterates graph nodes, derives doc paths, and builds two maps: one keyed
by doc path, one keyed by symbol name (for cross-referencing).

nodes.name: buildIndexResponse
nodes.description:
Build the docs index grouped by source directory.

Groups symbol entries by source directory and sorts both directories
and entries alphabetically for the sidebar tree.

nodes.name: buildDocResponse
nodes.description:
Build the doc response for a single symbol.

Generates markdown on-the-fly from graph data and enriches the response
with metadata from the symbol index. Returns the raw mermaid source for
the dependency graph (caller can render to SVG server-side if desired).

structuredParams.name: index
structuredParams.description: The symbol index for metadata enrichment

structuredParams.name: graph
structuredParams.description: The flow graph for rendering and mermaid generation

nodes.name: ContentHasher
nodes.description: SHA256 content hasher for detecting symbol changes. Hashes params + body (not name) so renames don't trigger staleness.

nodes.name: extractEventTrigger
nodes.description:
Extract the event name from an inngest.createFunction call.
Looks for patterns like: { event: "user/created" } or { event: "app/image.analyzed" }

nodes.name: extractFunctionId
nodes.description:
Extract the function ID from an inngest.createFunction call.
Looks for patterns like: { id: "analyze-image" }

nodes.name: extractRoutePath
nodes.description:
Extract route path from file path.
e.g. "src/app/api/analyze/route.ts" -> "/api/analyze"

nodes.name: extractPagePath
nodes.description:
Extract page path from file path.
e.g. "src/app/dashboard/page.tsx" -> "/dashboard"

nodes.name: getRouteMap
nodes.description:
Get or build a map from route paths to file paths.
Cached by reference equality on projectFiles — the graph builder passes
the same array for every connection, so this builds the map once per build.

structuredParams.name: projectFiles
structuredParams.description: all source file paths in the project

nodes.name: extractTaskId
nodes.description:
Extract the task ID from a task/schemaTask/schedules.task definition.
Looks for: id: "process-image" (with single, double, or backtick quotes)

nodes.name: extractCronSchedule
nodes.description:
Extract the cron schedule from a schedules.task() definition.
Handles both simple string form (`cron: "0 * * * *"`) and object form
(`cron: { pattern: "0 5 * * *", timezone: "Asia/Tokyo" }`).

nodes.name: handleChatRequest
nodes.description:
Handle a POST /api/chat request.

Streams responses from the Anthropic API with graph context,
automatically handling the tool-use loop for search_nodes and
select_nodes via the AI SDK.

nodes.name: serveStaticFile
nodes.description: Serve a static file with the appropriate Content-Type header.

nodes.name: startServer
nodes.description:
Start the treck documentation viewer HTTP server.

Serves the single-page viewer app, a JSON API for the symbol index and
individual doc pages, and the graph data. Watches graph.json for changes
and rebuilds the index automatically.

If the requested port is taken, retries up to 10 consecutive ports.

structuredParams.name: outputDir
structuredParams.description: Path to the treck output directory (e.g. `_treck`)

structuredParams.name: port
structuredParams.description: Preferred port number to listen on

throws: If no available port is found after 10 attempts

nodes.name: buildDocResponseWithSVG
nodes.description:
Build the doc response with server-side SVG rendering for the dependency graph.

Wraps the browser-safe `buildDocResponse` and replaces the raw mermaid
source with a rendered SVG string.

structuredParams.name: index
structuredParams.description: The symbol index for metadata enrichment

structuredParams.name: graph
structuredParams.description: The flow graph for rendering and mermaid generation

nodes.name: GraphExplorerWithData
nodes.description: Fetches graph data and renders the GraphExplorer.

nodes.name: App
nodes.description: Root application component for the treck viewer.

nodes.name: loadSettings
nodes.description: Load chat settings from localStorage.

nodes.name: saveSettings
nodes.description: Save chat settings to localStorage.

nodes.name: ChatMarkdown
nodes.description: Renders markdown content from assistant messages.

nodes.name: SettingsButton
nodes.description: Settings gear icon button.

nodes.name: CloseIcon
nodes.description: Close X icon.

nodes.name: ChatPanel
nodes.description: AI chat panel for code navigation questions.

nodes.name: kindToVariant
nodes.description: Maps a node kind to a badge variant.

nodes.name: entryTypeToVariant
nodes.description: Maps an entry type to a badge variant.

nodes.name: httpMethodToVariant
nodes.description: Maps an HTTP method to a badge variant.

nodes.name: DocContent
nodes.description: Renders doc content with structured badge metadata and parsed markdown body.

nodes.name: buildTree
nodes.description: Build a tree from the docs index, optionally filtering to only visible symbol names.

nodes.name: DocsTree
nodes.description: File tree navigation for browsing documented symbols.

nodes.name: DocsViewer
nodes.description: Full-page documentation reader, computing docs client-side from graph context.

nodes.name: FlowControls
nodes.description: Graph filtering controls: search, node stats, type filters, conditionals toggle.

nodes.name: getNodeCategory
nodes.description: Returns the filter category for a graph node (entry type or kind-based).

nodes.name: useDarkMode
nodes.description:
Detect dark mode by checking for a `.dark` class on the document root.

Observes class mutations on `<html>` so the value updates when the
theme is toggled at runtime.

nodes.name: FlowGraph
nodes.description: ReactFlow-based graph visualization with ELK layout.

nodes.name: GraphExplorer
nodes.description: Standalone graph explorer — renders sidebar, graph, and docs from a single graph prop.

nodes.name: useGraphExplorer
nodes.description: Access graph explorer context.

nodes.name: TrivialSymbolInfo
nodes.description: Subtle info card shown for trivial symbols where JSDoc isn't required.

nodes.name: MissingJsDocBanner
nodes.description: Banner shown when a symbol is missing its JSDoc comment, with a collapsible agent prompt.

nodes.name: ConditionNode
nodes.description: Angled-corner condition node (hexagonal shape via CSS clip-path on a background layer).

nodes.name: ThemeToggle
nodes.description: Theme toggle button that cycles through auto -> light -> dark.

nodes.name: ViewNav
nodes.description: Sidebar header with project name, theme toggle, and Graph/Docs navigation.

nodes.name: cleanConditionText
nodes.description:
Strip `if (...)` wrapper, trailing `&&`/`||`, and other noise from a
condition string to produce a clean expression for display.

For `else (expr)` conditions (else-only branches), negates the expression
so the condition node reads as the actual guard, e.g. `staleDocs.length > 0`.

nodes.name: negateExpression
nodes.description: Negate a simple comparison expression, or wrap in `!(...)`.

nodes.name: expandConditionals
nodes.description:
Expand conditional edges into condition-node chains that preserve nested
branch paths while still sharing common prefixes.

nodes.name: Badge
nodes.description: Renders a styled badge span as a React component.

nodes.name: docPathToUrl
nodes.description: Convert a docPath (e.g. "src/checker/index/StaleChecker.md") to a URL path

nodes.name: urlToDocPath
nodes.description: Convert a URL pathname back to a docPath for the API

nodes.name: escapeHtml
nodes.description: Escape HTML special characters to prevent XSS in rendered content.

nodes.name: snapCeil
nodes.description: Round a value up to the nearest multiple of SNAP_CEIL_SIZE (GRID_SIZE * 2).

nodes.name: getNextTheme
nodes.description: Returns the next theme preference in the cycle: auto -> light -> dark -> auto.

nodes.name: resolveMode
nodes.description: Resolve the effective mode from a preference, checking system setting for "auto".

nodes.name: applyThemePreference
nodes.description:
Apply a theme preference to the document root element.

Sets `data-theme`, `data-theme-preference`, the `color-scheme` CSS property,
and toggles the `dark` class. When preference is `"auto"`, resolves the
effective mode from the system `prefers-color-scheme` media query.

nodes.name: getActiveTheme
nodes.description:
Read the currently active theme from the document root.

Defaults to `"light"` on the server or when the attribute is absent.

nodes.name: getCurrentPreference
nodes.description:
Read the current theme preference from the DOM attribute.

Returns `"auto"` on the server or when no preference is found.

nodes.name: getStoredThemePreference
nodes.description:
Read the persisted theme preference from localStorage.

Returns `null` on the server or when no valid preference is stored.

nodes.name: storeThemePreference
nodes.description: Persist the theme preference to localStorage.

nodes.name: useThemePreference
nodes.description:
Manage the user's theme preference (light/dark/auto).

On mount, restores the preference from localStorage (or falls back to the
DOM value) and applies it. The returned `toggle` cycles through
auto -> light -> dark and persists the choice.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.description: Merge Tailwind CSS class names, resolving conflicts via `tailwind-merge`.

edges.label: else (url.pathname === '/api/graph') → else (url.pathname === '/api/index') → else (req.method === 'OPTIONS' && url.pathname === '/api/chat') → if (url.pathname === '/api/chat' && req.method === 'POST') → else (!graph)

else (url.pathname === '/api/graph') → else (url.pathname === '/api/index') → else (req.method === 'OPTIONS' && url.pathname === '/api/chat') → else (url.pathname === '/api/chat' && req.method === 'POST') → if (url.pathname === '/api/doc') → else (!docPath) → else (!graph)
